message:
fix(h1): flush framed write buffer after sending HTTP/1.1 response

Framed::send() only encodes the response into the internal write buffer
but does not flush it to the underlying socket. Without an explicit
poll_flush(), clients may hang waiting for response bytes that are
buffered but never transmitted, especially under low-traffic conditions
where no subsequent write triggers an implicit flush.

Add a poll_fn(|cx| framed.poll_flush(cx)).await call immediately after
send(), mapping any I/O error to HttpError::Io.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
